title: Design Your Frontend Like a Product, Not a Page
summary: A mental model for treating your UI as a living system with clear constraints and outcomes.

sections.title: Why Product Thinking Matters
sections.content:
Treat your frontend as a product with users, constraints, and a roadmap.

* Who is this UI for?*
* What problems are we actually solving?*
* How will we know it's working?*

sections.title: Systems Over Screens
sections.content:
Great frontends are systems: design tokens, layout primitives, typography scales, and interaction patterns.
Start small, name your decisions, and reuse relentlessly.

title: A Shipping Checklist for Frontend Engineers
summary: A calm pre-ship checklist you can reuse on every feature.

sections.title: UX Flow & Edge Cases
sections.content:
Walk the main flows slowly. Try weird inputs. Resize. Toggle dark mode.
If something feels off, it probably is.

sections.title: Accessibility & Semantics
sections.content:
Run through headings, labels, alt text, and keyboard navigation.
Use at least one automated check (like axe or Lighthouse).
